coord_build: not_reported (build not stated in paper; OCA2 exon 17, melanosomal cytoplasmic domain; His615 conserved GERP 366)
pvalue: 1.75e-8 (buttock, constitutive); 5.34e-6 (underarm, constitutive); 5.12e-4 (hand back, facultative) — all survive Bonferroni; melanocyte t-test AA vs GG P=0.015, AG vs GG P=0.016
evidence_quote: An amino acid substitution (His615Arg) of OCA2 prevalent in most East Asian populations—but absent in Africans and Europeans—was significantly associated with skin lightening among northern Han Chinese.
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; H615R missense, validated via CRISPR/Cas9 mouse knock-in His610Arg.
author_explanation_quote: rs1800414 is a nonsynonymous SNP located in exon 17 of OCA2, and an A to G mutation causes an amino acid change from histidine to arginine (His615Arg). This missense mutation is located in the melanosomal cytoplasmic topological domain
notes: Additionally validated with a CRISPR/Cas9 mouse knock-in model (His610Arg) demonstrating melanin productivity effect -- strong mechanism_proposed status.

coord_build: not_reported (within the ~44.2 kb OCA2 CHB-vs-A-A divergence peak)
pvalue: 0.0395 marginal (buttock only); 0.632 when conditioned on rs1800414
evidence_quote: Signal attributable to LD with rs1800414 (conditional analysis). Not an independent effect.
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; LD-dependent on rs1800414 (idx94), became nonsignificant P=0.632 when conditioned -- passenger.
author_explanation_quote: with a marginal significance for skin on the buttock (P = 0.0395)...When conditioning for rs1800414, rs12440942 became nonsignificant (P = 0.632)
notes: Authors explicitly explain this SNP's association as non-independent of (a passenger of) rs1800414 via conditional analysis -- a mechanistic account of non-independence, not merely a bare unexplained association.

population: Europeans (literature-cited from Lamason et al. 2005; not ge
evidence_quote: the SLC24A5 Ala111Thr variant is nearly fixed in Europeans (>95%) by positive selection but very rare in Africans and East Asians.
classification_notes: SLC24A5 canonical; Ala111Thr, cited from Lamason 2005 as background/contrast, not a novel finding of this paper.
author_explanation_quote: The Ala111Thr variant of SLC24A5 proved the most striking, being nearly fixed in Europeans (>95%) due to local Darwinian positive selection, but extremely rare in Africans and East Asians
notes: Cited as established background/contrast case (light-skin variant in Europeans, absent in East Asians) rather than a novel finding of this paper; mechanism (missense, positive selection) is attributed to the cited literature (Lamason et al. 2005), which Yang2016 restates approvingly.
